title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google's Chrome team that replaces DOM-scraping and pixel-based interactions with structured tools. It provides declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs that let agents call named, typed, described actions directly on websites.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard for exposing structured tools and actions from websites directly to AI agents, using either declarative HTML attributes or imperative JavaScript APIs. It matters because it offers an alternative to DOM traversal and pixel-based interaction, the brittle methods agents currently rely on to operate web interfaces. The core takeaway: sites can publish named, typed, and described actions that agents call directly, replacing screen-scraping and visual navigation with explicit interfaces.
